content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a light/reflection phenomenon and a deliberate metacognitive act of learning in high school literacy curricula, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to integrate the dual core of reflexion: enabling large language models to not only reflect on their outputs like bouncing light off a surface, but also engage in structured, curriculum-style deliberate reflexion to build long-term generalizable reasoning ability, similar to how human students practice reflexive writing to improve critical thinking.
